story.asA: a developer implementing the Recipe conversion engine
story.iWant: a robust lrtemplate Lua parser that extracts all parameters from Lightroom Classic preset files into UniversalRecipe data
story.soThat: users can convert Lightroom Classic presets to other formats (NP3, XMP) with 95%+ accuracy
story.tasks: - Task 1: Design Lua parsing strategy with regex patterns (AC: FR-1) - Task 2: Implement core Parse() function (AC: FR-1, FR-2, FR-3) - Task 3: Implement HSL color extraction (AC: FR-4) - Task 4: Implement advanced features extraction (AC: FR-5) - Task 5: Implement error handling with ConversionError (AC: FR-7, NFR-3) - Task 6: Implement string and character handling (AC: FR-1, FR-6) - Task 7: Write comprehensive tests (AC: NFR-2) - Task 8: Documentation and code quality (AC: NFR-3)

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### Functional Requirements **FR-1: Lua Table Parsing** - Parse valid lrtemplate files starting with `s = {`, handle escaped characters, extract 50+ parameters using regex, return clear errors for invalid syntax, support multi-line formats
**FR-2: Core Parameter Extraction** - Extract Exposure (-5.0 to +5.0), Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Whites, Blacks (all -100 to +100)
**FR-3: Color Parameter Extraction** - Extract Saturation, Vibrance, Clarity (-100 to +100), Sharpness (0 to 150), Temperature (2000 to 50000K), Tint (-150 to +150)
**FR-4: HSL Color Adjustments** - Extract HSL for 8 colors (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta) with Hue/Saturation/Luminance (-100 to +100 each)
**FR-5: Advanced Features** - Extract ToneCurvePV2012 array with coordinate pairs, Split Toning parameters (Shadow/Highlight Hue 0-360, Saturation 0-100)
**FR-6: Data Type Handling** - Parse floats and integers correctly, handle missing fields gracefully with zero values, validate numeric ranges, handle negative numbers
**FR-7: Error Handling** - Clear errors for invalid syntax, wrap all errors in ConversionError with context (Operation="parse", Format="lrtemplate"), include field names in errors
### Non-Functional Requirements **NFR-1: Performance** - Parse single file in <20ms, pre-compile regex patterns for efficiency
**NFR-2: Test Coverage** - Parse all 566 lrtemplate samples without errors, ≥90% test coverage, round-trip validation (±1 tolerance)
**NFR-3: Code Quality** - Follow Pattern 4 (file structure), Pattern 5 (error handling), Pattern 6 (inline validation), Pattern 7 (table-driven tests), use stdlib regex only, pass gofmt and go vet

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Defines Parse() implementation using regex-based Lua table parsing with pre-compiled patterns for efficiency. Must validate Lua syntax starting with 's = {', extract 50+ parameters matching XMP fields, and handle escaped characters in quoted strings.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: All conversion failures must be wrapped in ConversionError{Operation, Format, Cause} type. For parser: Operation="parse", Format="lrtemplate", with optional Field parameter for debugging context.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: lrtemplate parse target: <20ms. Pre-compile regex patterns for efficiency (avoid repeated compilation), which can represent 50%+ of parse time.

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.section: Pattern 4: File Structure for Format Packages
doc.snippet: Every format package has identical structure: parse.go, generate.go, {format}_test.go. lrtemplate package follows same pattern as np3 and xmp.

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: Always return wrapped ConversionError for conversion failures with Operation, Format, and Cause fields for type-safe error handling and clear error messages.

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: Validate inline in parsers, fail fast with descriptive errors. Check file format, syntax, and parameter bounds immediately upon parsing.

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: Table-driven tests using 1,501 real sample files from testdata/. Round-trip tests ensure 95%+ accuracy goal. Test coverage ≥90% required.

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Parse Lua table syntax correctly, handle quoted strings and escaped characters, generate lrtemplate files that load in Lightroom Classic, maintain parameter parity with XMP (50+ fields).

artifact.reason: ConversionError type definition - MUST reuse this exact type for lrtemplate parser errors. Located at xmp/parse.go:40-61 with Operation, Format, Field, and Cause fields.
Reference implementation of Parse() function signature and error handling pattern. Shows validate-then-extract pattern with fail-fast approach.
Table-driven test pattern using filepath.Glob to discover test files. Shows how to structure tests with testdata/ directory and t.Run subtests.
Target data structure for parser. Contains all 50+ fields including Exposure, Contrast, Temperature, HSL colors. Parser must populate this struct.
Struct for HSL adjustments (Hue, Saturation, Luminance) used for 8 colors in UniversalRecipe. Parser must extract these for Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta.
Another parser reference showing file structure and error handling patterns. Demonstrates binary format parsing vs text-based parsing for lrtemplate.

package.name: regexp
package.usage: Pre-compiled regex patterns for Lua table parsing

package.name: strconv
package.usage: String to int/float conversion for parsed values

go.note: Zero external dependencies - stdlib only per architectural requirement

story-context.constraints: - MUST reuse ConversionError type from internal/formats/xmp/parse.go (lines 40-61) - DO NOT recreate this type - MUST wrap all parser errors with Operation="parse", Format="lrtemplate" - MUST follow Pattern 4: File structure (parse.go in internal/formats/lrtemplate/) - MUST follow Pattern 5: Error handling with ConversionError wrapper - MUST follow Pattern 6: Inline validation, fail fast on invalid input - MUST follow Pattern 7: Table-driven tests with real sample files - MUST use stdlib regex package only (no external Lua libraries or interpreters) - MUST pre-compile regex patterns as package-level variables for performance - MUST NOT execute Lua code - regex-based parsing only for security - MUST validate file starts with "s = {" prefix before parsing - MUST handle escaped characters in quoted strings (backslashes, quotes, newlines) - MUST support both single-line and multi-line Lua table formats - MUST populate SourceFormat field in UniversalRecipe with "lrtemplate" - MUST achieve <20ms parse time (validated via benchmarks) - MUST achieve ≥90% test coverage - MUST pass gofmt and go vet without warnings

interface.name: ConversionError
interface.signature: type ConversionError struct { Operation string; Format string; Field string; Cause error }

interface.name: UniversalRecipe
interface.signature: type UniversalRecipe struct with 50+ fields (Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Whites, Blacks, Saturation, Vibrance, Temperature, Tint, HSL colors, etc.)

tests.standards: Follow Pattern 7 (Testing Strategy) from architecture documentation. Use table-driven tests with real sample files from testdata/lrtemplate/ directory (566 files). Each test should use filepath.Glob to discover test files and t.Run for subtests. Validate parser output against expected UniversalRecipe fields. Round-trip tests are PRIMARY validation strategy: lrtemplate → parse → generate → parse should produce identical results (±1 tolerance for rounding). Benchmarks must validate <20ms performance target. Test coverage must be ≥90% measured with go test -cover. Follow same patterns as internal/formats/xmp/xmp_test.go for consistency.

test.description: TestParse - Table-driven test using filepath.Glob to parse all 566 lrtemplate sample files from testdata/lrtemplate/
TestParseInvalidPrefix - Verify error returned when file doesn't start with "s = {"
TestParseBasicParameters - Validate extraction of Exposure, Contrast, Highlights, Shadows, Whites, Blacks, Saturation, Vibrance, etc.
TestParseHSLColors - Validate extraction of HSL adjustments for all 8 colors (Red, Orange, Yellow, Green, Aqua, Blue, Purple, Magenta)
TestParseToneCurve - Validate parsing of ToneCurvePV2012 array with nested coordinate pairs
TestParseSplitToning - Validate extraction of Split Toning parameters (Shadow/Highlight Hue and Saturation)
TestParseDataTypes - Verify correct parsing of floats (Exposure), integers (Contrast), and negative numbers
TestParseMissingFields - Verify graceful handling of missing fields with zero values in UniversalRecipe
TestParseInvalidSyntax - Verify ConversionError returned for malformed Lua syntax with proper context
TestParseEscapedCharacters - Validate handling of escaped characters in quoted strings (backslashes, quotes, newlines)
TestRoundTrip - Parse lrtemplate → UniversalRecipe → Generate → Parse again, compare original vs recovered (±1 tolerance)
BenchmarkParse - Benchmark parsing performance to validate <20ms target
TestParseEdgeCases - Empty file, truncated file, invalid UTF-8, very large values
